enrichment.summary: Kyle logs a typical day in Boulder: a 12.5-mile bike commute, a frustrated visit to his mom's house over a phone call he couldn't complete, and an evening spent iterating on his website. He also shares his new habit of listening to a different audiobook each day at work, mentioning the book 'Life in a Black Regiment.'
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: prefers cycling through different audiobooks rather than repeating the same one — more exciting
found the day's design work frustrating but worthwhile — kept changing things until something felt right

transcript.text: Good morning, living in Boulder Vlog, and for the 16th of June, 2014, I guess we're halfway through June.
It's finally starting to warm up, that's nice.
I rode my bike like 12 and a half miles to the first house yesterday, and then went over to another one,
Then came home, or went to my mom's house, because she wanted me to help her organize her office,
But I mentioned taking my life home. I was out, and she made me call Medicaid to see if I could get it taken out,
But I couldn't get through, which frustrated me, so I ended up not wanting to help her.
I just came home, and then was working on my computer, but on my website.
But it was basically a day of just figuring out what I wanted it to look like. It was weird.
Like, I went through fixing like 80 different things, and then, I can't give her the details right now,
But 80 different things, like over and over again, didn't realize I didn't like it that way until I was actually like that.
Anyways, I'm listening to audiobooks. I'm doing this new thing where I listen to an audiobook every day.
See, I've been working now for a bit, a month and a half now, so I'm getting into a groove,
So the groove I'm excited about is a new audiobook every day.
Before, I was just doing the same one over and over again, but now I'm doing a different one,
Which is really exciting, because some books are just fascinating.
Others, you just want to get through once and never do again, but some are just really good.
Yesterday, I did one called Life in a Black Regiment.
It was this white guy who was the commander of the first black regiment in the Civil War, and that was good.
But I have a whole bunch of other audiobooks on my MP3 that I just put on, so I'll choose one and get through it.
My rule of thumb is if I don't make it through the whole audiobook, like at work,
Well, then I finish it at home, but even if it does finish at work, then I just listen to it.
It loops over, and I keep listening to it, and then when I get home, I just put another one on.
Or if I'm halfway through one, then I just start where I was, you know.
But, yeah.
Anyways, I'm excited to, I have to ride back from Superior today, so it's kind of another commute coming home.
And then I get home, I'm excited to get home and see how far I can get on my website.
Or this webpage, rather.
And that's about all.
Okay, peace out.
